intended_use: Transpara Density is a software application intended for use with data from compatible digital mammography and digital breast tomosynthesis systems. Transpara Density utilizes deep learning artificial
intended_use_full: Transpara Density is a software application intended for use with data from compatible digital mammography and digital breast tomosynthesis systems. Transpara Density utilizes deep learning artificial intelligence algorithms to automatically determine volumetric breast density (VBD), breast volume, and an ACR BI-RADS 5th Edition breast density category to aid health care professionals in the assessment of breast tissue composition. It is not a diagnostic aid.
target_condition: Breast density assessment in mammography and breast tomosynthesis
ground_truth: Panel majority vote of eight MQSA-qualified radiologists according to ACR BI-RADS Atlas 5th Edition for agreement testing; physics-based model from van Engeland et al. (2006) for accuracy of volumetric measurements; breast MRI measurements for volumetric breast density validation
other_metrics: Cohen's weighted kappa = 0.78 [95% CI: 0.72 - 0.84] for dense vs non-dense; Cohen's weighted kappa = 0.74 [95% CI: 0.70 - 0.79] for four categories. Pearson correlation with physics-based model: 0.935 [95% CI: 0.931 - 0.938]. Pearson correlation with breast MRI: 0.908 [95% CI: 0.878 - 0.931]. Reproducibility: correlation between CC and MLO views 0.947 [95% CI: 0.945 - 0.948], correlation between left and right breast 0.953 [95% CI: 0.951 - 0.955]. DBT/FFDM comparison: correlation 0.912 [95% CI: 0.904 - 0.920], quadratically weighted kappa 0.810 [95% CI: 0.787 - 0.835].
